purpose: Mining pass to raise the page-58 stance gold contradicts class above n=1 (the v3 set held only stance2b-001). 278 candidates panelled; 3 surfaced as draft-contradicts (1.1%).
note: final_label is Papa-authored and overrides draft_label. label_source for these rows := papa.

decisions.rationale: Same satellite/central denial Papa already confirmed as stance2b-001 in the v3 set. Re-found here as a sanity check; not a new row, does not increment the class.

Paper (Piotrowska 2022 / Bluck 2023) names black hole mass as the key predictor of intrinsic quenching via feedback energy. Papa: this does NOT deny stellar-mass-as-primary-driver, because BH mass and bulge/stellar mass are tightly coupled (M_BH-M_bulge relation) and AGN feedback is itself part of stellar-mass-regulated quenching. The paper names an underlying mechanism that co-varies with the stated primary driver, not a competing one. No denial -> related_different_facet.

Same paper, barer wording ('black hole mass by far the most predictive parameter in simulations and SDSS'). Same Papa reasoning as mine-024: BH-mass-primary co-varies with stellar-mass-primary via the BH-bulge relation, so it is a complementary/underlying mechanism, not a denial. Note: qwen independently reached this exact label and reason; only Claude's tiebreak pushed it to contradicts. -> related_different_facet.

boundary_principle_refinement: Extends the v3 denial test: a paper that names a DIFFERENT primary variable does NOT contradict the base when that variable is physically degenerate / tightly co-varying with the base's named driver (e.g. black hole mass vs stellar/bulge mass via M_BH-M_bulge). The denial test must be applied with domain knowledge of variable degeneracy, not syntactically. Claude's tiebreak false-positived mine-024/066 by reading 'names a different driver' as denial; the domain truth is the drivers co-vary -> related_different_facet.

outcome.corpus_verdict: slice-2b exhausted for contradicts. 1.1% surface rate, and both model-surfaced candidates overturned by domain expert. A third mine will not move it.
outcome.methodology_finding: Panel+Claude-tiebreak over-calls contradicts: Claude alone pushed 024/066 to contradicts (0.82/0.78) over gpt=unrelated 1.0; domain expert (BH-bulge co-variance) overturned both. qwen independently got 066 right. Human sign-off gate validated; the gate-under-test carries a contradicts-over-call (precision) risk.
outcome.row_quality_note: mine-024/066 are expert-ambiguous: the source papers' thesis is BH-mass primacy, which is precisely why the panel leaned contradicts. Defensible both ways -> weak gold rows. Recommend keeping them OUT of the scored set; preserve here as documented evidence the corpus was searched.

open_decision: Lock the page-58 stance gold set. Recommended terms: (1) lock the v3 129-row set with Papa's 3 adjudications folded (000->facet, 001->contradicts, 003->facet); (2) contradicts stays n=1 -> scored as a documented 1-row sentinel (smoke-test), not a scored F1 class; (3) mine-024/066 stay OUT of the scored set (expert-ambiguous, biased denial-cue draw) -> locked set = 129. Optional toggle: include 024/066 as a tagged hard-negative facet subset that probes the contradicts-over-call failure mode.
